title: Orrk
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Guildmaster_orrk.jpg]] |name=Orrk |homeworld=[[Vandelhelm/Legends|Vandelhelm]][[Star Wars (1977) 98|''Star Wars'' (1977) 98]] |birth= |death=[[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Vandelhelm |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Gray]] |feathers= |eyes=Black |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Metalsmiths Guild]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I don't trust Orrk. He's been working with the Imperials for over a decade, and near as I can tell, he pretty much runs the place now.|Lando Calrissian|{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20040304vandelhelm|text=Vandelhelm: Enemies and Alloys|int=Vandelhelm: Enemies and Alloys}}}} '''Orrk''' was [[Guild Master/Legends|Guildmaster]] of the [[Metalsmiths Guild]] of [[Vandelhelm/Legends|Vandelhelm]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. A longtime [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] collaborator, Orrk continued to conspire with Imperial [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] [[Mordur]] in the aftermath of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], attempting to keep Vandelhelm's valuable metal production from benefiting the [[Alliance of Free Planets]]. Orrk attempted to kidnap Vandelhelm's [[Venerated Ones]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], but was gunned down by Alliance war hero [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. ==Biography== {{Quote|Why take chances?"
"Because it's the best way to win, Solo.|Han Solo and Orrk|}} A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Orrk served as [[Guild Master/Legends|Guildmaster]] of the [[Metalsmiths Guild]] on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Vandelhelm/Legends|Vandelhelm]], a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] known for its importance to the shipbuilding trade. A [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] conspirator for over a decade, Orrk retained his position as Guildmaster after the Empire's climactic defeat at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], but his past loyalties made some within the [[Alliance of Free Planets]]—including [[General/Legends|General]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], who had dealt with Orrk during his time as [[Baron Administrator/Legends|Baron Administrator]] of [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]'s [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]]—suspicious. As it happened, the Alliance was right to be leery of Orrk. Seeking to keep Vandelhelm's metals away from Alliance control, Orrk conspired with an Imperial [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] named [[Mordur]]—whom he sheltered aboard a converted [[Drone barge/Legends|drone barge]]—to [[Prison/Legends|imprison]] the Venerated Ones two child royals who held great symbolic significance to the world's metalsmiths, in an Imperial internment facility on [[Ord Vaug]]. With the Venerated Ones gone, Orrk announced that the Metalsmiths Guild was ceasing production until their return, denying the Alliance the alloys it used to augment its fleet. Orrk's plan took a hit when Alliance forces liberated Ord Vaug and freed the Venerated Ones, dispatching war heroes [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Nien Nunb/Legends|Nien Nunb]] to ferry the children back to Vandelhelm. Orrk and Mordur planned to resolve this issue by simply killing the Venerated Ones upon their [[Mission to Vandelhelm|return]] to the [[Star system/Legends|system]], but Solo and Nunb deftly [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] the ''[[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Millennium Falcon]]'' through a [[Mine/Legends|mined]] [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid belt]] to bring them back to Vandelhelm. Orrk returned planetside to greet Solo and the Venerated Ones and find a way to regain control of the situation, ultimately electing to make an attempt on the children's lives. Orrk took the Venerated Ones hostage and attempted to force them onto Solo's ''[[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Millennium Falcon]]'', only for Solo—famously quick on the draw—to gun Orrk down and save the children. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|No wonder your side lost the war!|Orrk, to Mordur|}} Orrk was a longtime, committed Imperial loyalist, one who reveled in the control his position bestowed upon the Metalsmiths Guild. However, after the Empire's defeat at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] he began to display some contempt for his Imperial allies, making comments about how they lost the Galactic Civil War. Orrk ably and disingenuously wore a public face as the dedicated head of the guild, but among guild members, Orrk had a reputation of being distant and uninterested in their concerns, only deigning to visit them when he had further consolidated his power. Indeed, Orrk had a strong resentment for the metalsmiths he governed, thinking of them as superstitious and simple. ==Behind the scenes== Orrk appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 98|''Star Wars'' 98]], written by [[Archie Goodwin]], illustrated by [[Al Williamson]] and released in [[1985]]. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars (1977) 98|''Star Wars'' (1977) 98]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 98}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' {{Imo}} *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20040304vandelhelm|text=Vandelhelm: Enemies and Alloys|int=Vandelhelm: Enemies and Alloys}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Government figures]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]]
